verdict.answer_first: B states the core actionable definition earlier: “An API is RESTful if it satisfies a specific set of constraints”; A opens with a contrast before naming the criterion.
verdict.decisiveness: A commits more cleanly to the practical distinction: “strict sense... hypermedia” vs “everyday sense... resource modeling and correct use of HTTP semantics,” while B adds a follow-up prompt.
verdict.actionability: A is easier to act on right now because it ends with the strict-vs-practical checklist and names the exact everyday REST conventions to implement.
